text:
I want to run this on 0.0.0.0:8000 to accept any incoming connections: ```gunicorn -w2 -t120 --reload cloud.wsgi```


To make Gunicorn accept connections from any IP address on port 8000, add the bind parameter like this:

```bash
gunicorn -w2 -t120 --reload --bind 0.0.0.0:8000 cloud.wsgi
```

This command:
- `-w2`: Runs 2 worker processes
- `-t120`: Sets timeout to 120 seconds
- `--reload`: Auto-reloads on code changes
- `--bind 0.0.0.0:8000`: Binds to all available network interfaces on port 8000
- `cloud.wsgi`: Your WSGI application

Now Gunicorn will accept connections from any IP address on port 8000, not just localhost.

Note: Make sure your firewall settings allow incoming connections on port 8000 if you're running this on a remote server.
